name: Google Now
short_description: A proactive context-aware assistant that delivered unrequested 'cards' based on location, calendar, search history, and time-of-day — the first mainstream push-model ambient assistant.
long_description: Google Now launched with Android 4.1 'Jelly Bean' in July 2012 as the first mainstream system to fuse location, calendar appointments, search history, and time-of-day into a continuous stream of proactively delivered information cards. Rather than waiting to be asked, it predicted what users needed — a boarding pass surfaced before a flight, commute traffic before the morning departure, a weather card as a storm approached — defining the ambient push model of context-aware computing. Popular Science named it 'Innovation of the Year' for 2012. The 'Google Now' branding was phased out around 2016 in favour of the Google Feed (later Discover), and its two-way-conversation successor, Google Assistant, was announced at Google I/O 2016.
ai_note: Uses machine learning to rank and surface personalized cards from fused signals — search history, location patterns, calendar data, and behavioral signals — entirely without explicit user queries.

images.caption: A Nexus 7 tablet displaying Google Now cards, photographed August 2012 shortly after the service launched.
images.alt_text: A Nexus 7 tablet held in a hand showing the Google Now interface, with a scrollable feed of contextual information cards on screen.

sources.title: Google Now

sources.title: Google Now Named Popular Science Innovation of the Year 2012
sources.access_notes: Names Google Now as 'Innovation of the Year' for its proactive context-aware card system.

interactions.title: A boarding pass that appears before you ask
interactions.description: Gmail detects a flight-confirmation email; Google Now surfaces a boarding-pass card with gate, seat, and departure time on the lock screen the morning of the flight — without the user searching for anything.

interactions.title: Traffic warning that leaves before the jam
interactions.description: The system reads the user's recurring calendar appointments, infers the usual commute window, and pushes a travel-time card that warns 'leave 15 minutes early today' before gridlock peaks — a prediction rather than a query.

interactions.title: Sports score for a team you never declared
interactions.description: By observing which sports-related searches a user ran and which teams they had Googled, Now learned team allegiances and surfaced live scores and match outcomes without any explicit sports preferences being set.

interactions.title: Package tracking from an email you forgot you got
interactions.description: Now scanned Gmail for shipping confirmations and, as the delivery date approached, posted a tracking card with the latest courier status — surfacing information the user possessed but had not thought to check.

interactions.title: Weather card timed to your leaving
interactions.description: Using a combination of location, calendar events, and typical departure times learned from movement patterns, Now surfaced a rain warning at the moment it mattered — right before the user was likely to walk out the door — rather than on a fixed morning schedule.

interactions.title: Discovering a restaurant nearby when you were already looking
interactions.description: Frequent restaurant searchers in a given area found that Now began surfacing dining suggestions with hours, rating, and walking distance as they moved through a neighborhood — an inferred intent card from location and search-history fusion.

interactions.title: A card that knows you changed your mind
interactions.description: Building on the system's existing awareness of calendar events and location history, a future iteration could surface a quiet 'you might be running late — do you want to send a message?' card as the user lingers far from a meeting location — proactive social coordination rather than only information delivery.
